title: Help-Seeking Introduction (GHSQ)
description: Explore barriers to seeking mental health support in Egyptian culture. Learn about the General Help-Seeking Questionnaire framework.
descriptionAr: استكشف عوائق طلب الدعم النفسي في الثقافة المصرية. تعرف على إطار استبيان طلب المساعدة العام.

exercises.scenario: Ahmad, a 25-year-old from Cairo, has been feeling persistently sad for 3 months. His family tells him 'Just pray more, you'll be fine.' He hasn't told anyone about his suicidal thoughts.
exercises.scenarioAr: أحمد، شاب عمره ٢٥ سنة من القاهرة، يشعر بحزن مستمر منذ ٣ أشهر. عائلته تقول له 'صلّي أكتر وهتبقى كويس.' لم يخبر أحداً بأفكاره الانتحارية.
barriersToSeekingHelp: Lack of awareness about professional mental health services
barriersAr: نقص الوعي بخدمات الصحة النفسية المهنية
appropriateResources: WHO Mental Health Gap Action Programme (mhGAP)
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) from licensed psychologist
Islamic counseling that integrates with professional help
exercises.ghsqInterpretation: Ahmad's GHSQ score would likely show low help-seeking intention across all categories. Intervention should address stigma, integrate Islamic values WITH professional care, and provide accessible, confidential pathways.
exercises.islamicPerspective: The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) said: 'Make use of medical treatment, for Allah has not made a disease without appointing a remedy for it' (Abu Dawud 3855). Seeking professional mental health care is consistent with Islamic teaching on taking practical action alongside prayer.

exercises.scenario: Fatima, a 30-year-old teacher in Upper Egypt, experiences severe anxiety attacks. Her mother-in-law insists she needs a 'sheikh to read Quran over her' and accuses her of weak faith.
exercises.scenarioAr: فاطمة، معلمة عمرها ٣٠ سنة في صعيد مصر، تعاني من نوبات قلق شديدة. حماتها تصر أنها تحتاج 'شيخ يقرأ عليها قرآن' وتتهمها بضعف الإيمان.
barriersToSeekingHelp: Family authority overriding personal health decisions
Geographic barrier (limited services in Upper Egypt)
barriersAr: سلطة العائلة تتغلب على قرارات الصحة الشخصية
appropriateResources: Behman Hospital Psychiatric Center (can provide remote consultations)
exercises.ghsqInterpretation: Fatima's barriers are multilayered: cultural, geographic, gendered, and economic. Intervention must engage the family system, validate spiritual practices WHILE adding professional care, and leverage telehealth for geographic access.
exercises.islamicPerspective: Ruqyah (Quranic healing) and professional therapy are NOT mutually exclusive. Imam al-Ghazali in 'Ihya Ulum al-Din' discussed the health of the nafs (self) as requiring both spiritual AND practical intervention. True tawakkul (trust in Allah) includes taking practical steps.

exercises.scenario: Omar, a 19-year-old university student, has been experiencing depression since failing his exams. He believes depression is a sign of weakness and tells himself 'real men don't feel this way.'
exercises.scenarioAr: عمر، طالب جامعي عمره ١٩ سنة، يعاني من الاكتئاب منذ رسوبه في الامتحانات. يعتقد أن الاكتئاب علامة ضعف ويقول لنفسه 'الرجالة الحقيقية ما بتحسّش كده.'
barriersToSeekingHelp: Lack of campus mental health resources
Belief that depression is a choice
barriersAr: نقص موارد الصحة النفسية في الحرم الجامعي
appropriateResources: National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) resources
CBT self-help books (e.g., Feeling Good by David Burns)
exercises.ghsqInterpretation: Omar's toxic masculinity beliefs create a major barrier. Psychoeducation about depression as a medical condition (not a character flaw) is essential. Male-friendly mental health campaigns and peer support could increase his help-seeking intention.
exercises.islamicPerspective: Multiple prophets experienced deep sadness: Prophet Ya'qub (Jacob) wept so much over Yusuf that he lost his sight (Quran 12:84). Prophet Muhammad himself experienced 'Am al-Huzn' (Year of Sorrow). Depression is NOT a sign of weak faith — it's a human experience acknowledged in our tradition.

exercises.scenario: Nadia, a 40-year-old accountant, recognizes she needs therapy for her chronic anxiety but worries: 'If anyone at work finds out, I'll lose my job.'
exercises.scenarioAr: نادية، محاسبة عمرها ٤٠ سنة، تدرك أنها تحتاج علاجاً نفسياً لقلقها المزمن لكنها تقلق: 'لو حد في الشغل عرف، هفقد شغلي.'
appropriateResources: Private practice psychologists (not connected to employer)
exercises.ghsqInterpretation: Nadia has high help-seeking awareness but practical barriers around confidentiality. Solutions should emphasize the legal protections for mental health patients, the confidentiality of therapy, and private service options.
exercises.islamicPerspective: Islam mandates the preservation of reputation (hifdh al-'ird) as part of the Maqasid al-Shariah. Seeking confidential mental health care fulfills BOTH the preservation of intellect (hifdh al-'aql) AND reputation simultaneously.
